


yyyy/mmdd(or -ddd)/hh.h):2024/-286/11.0UT  geog Lat/Long/Alt= 91.0/   0.0/ 400.0

NeQuick is used for topside Ne profile
OTSB2012 plasmasphere model w/o  plasmapause
URSI maps are used for the F2 peak density (NmF2)
foF2 STORM model is turned on 
AMTB-2013 model is used for F2 peak height (hmF2)
ABT-2009 option is used for the bottomside thickness parameter B0
Scotto-97 no L   option is used for the F1 occurrence probability
foE auroral storm model is turned off
IRI-1990   option is used for D-region
TBT-2012 with solar dependence is used for the electron temperature
TBKS2021 option is used for the ion temperature
RBV10+TBT15 option is used for ion composition
CGM coordinates not computed
auroral boundaries not computed

Solar and magnetic parameter for the 1st profile point:
Solar Zenith Angle/degree                              81.6
Dip (Magnetic Inclination)/degree                     -72.85
Modip (Modified Dip)/degree                           -90.00
Solar Sunspot Number (12-months running mean) Rz12    139.6           
Ionospheric-Effective Solar Index IG12                133.2           
Solar radio flux F10.7 (daily)                        212.6           
Solar radio flux F10.7 (81-day average)               205.0           

TEC [1.E16 m-2] is obtained by numerical integration in 1km steps
 from    50.0 to  2000.0 km. t is the percentage of TEC above the F peak.
